mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding labor income and aggregate demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not positive in more than 50% of months when PAYEMS increases month-over-month over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5%, restrictive credit conditions raise the cost of capital and reduce equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS > 2.5%, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales coinciding with declining industrial production indicate a demand-supply imbalance where consumption outpaces production capacity, squeezing corporate margins and triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When RSAFS rises and INDPRO falls simultaneously, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in fewer than 45% of occurrences over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In high-employment regimes where the unemployment rate is below 4%, further declines in unemployment fail to stimulate demand and instead signal labor market overheating that precedes monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods with UNRATE below 4.0%, further month-over-month declines in UNRATE are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real private residential construction permits predicts future housing-driven consumption and credit expansion, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: After two consecutive monthly increases in HOUST, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation accelerates alongside rising unemployment, stagflation fears drive risk premia higher and compress equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month change > 0.5% and UNRATE rises month-over-month
failure_condition: During months when CPIAUCSL accelerates and UNRATE rises together, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals broad-based economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not positive following quarters of positive GDPC1 growth in more than 55% of instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in industrial production indicates weakening manufacturing momentum and inventory destocking that precedes downward earnings revisions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO contracts by more than 0.5% month-over-month, S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.
